candidate_evaluations.data_source_terms_risk: Medium: it wraps multiple public baseball data sites/APIs. Consumers must respect each upstream source's terms, rate limits, attribution, and cache expectations before production activation.
candidate_evaluations.fit_for_betting_repo: Best fit for historical/statcast adoption spike because it covers the requested statcast/historical surface without custom MLB scraping logic.
candidate_evaluations.maintenance_release_evidence: PyPI shows pybaseball 2.2.7 uploaded on 2023-09-08; the GitHub repository is public with issue/PR history visible as of 2026-07-01.
candidate_evaluations.purpose: Python package for historical baseball data including Statcast, FanGraphs, Baseball Reference, and player/team lookup helpers.
candidate_evaluations.python_compatibility: PyPI classifiers list Python 3.8, 3.9, 3.10, and 3.11. Local .venv import smoke found pybaseball 2.2.7 available on Python 3.13, but this task does not add or pin the dependency.

candidate_evaluations.data_source_terms_risk: Medium: delegates to MLB Stats API/public MLB endpoints. MLB copyright and acceptable-use language must be reviewed before production use.
candidate_evaluations.fit_for_betting_repo: Functionally useful for read-only team, schedule, and player metadata, but GPL-3.0 licensing requires Owner/license review before dependency add.
candidate_evaluations.maintenance_release_evidence: PyPI shows version 1.9.0 released on 2025-04-04; GitHub repository is public and remains a recognized wrapper for statsapi-style team/schedule/player access as of 2026-07-01.
candidate_evaluations.purpose: Python wrapper for MLB Stats API endpoints covering teams, schedule, players, standings, and game metadata.
candidate_evaluations.python_compatibility: PyPI supports Python 3 and typical modern CPython usage; not currently installed in this repo's system python or .venv.

candidate_evaluations.data_source_terms_risk: Medium: also relies on MLB Stats API/public MLB endpoints, so MLB terms, copyright, rate limits, and caching boundaries apply.
candidate_evaluations.fit_for_betting_repo: Possible object-oriented alternative for normalized team/player metadata, but not preferred over pybaseball plus MLB-StatsAPI for this spike.
candidate_evaluations.maintenance_release_evidence: PyPI shows version 0.7.2 uploaded on 2026-02-05; GitHub repository is public and should still be reviewed before any dependency add.
candidate_evaluations.purpose: Object-oriented Python client around MLB Stats API data models for teams, players, games, stats, and related metadata.
candidate_evaluations.python_compatibility: PyPI requires Python >=3.10 and classifiers list Python 3.10 through 3.14; not currently installed in this repo's system python or .venv.

candidate_evaluations.data_source_terms_risk: Medium: wraps public baseball data sources with the same upstream terms and rate-limit concerns.
candidate_evaluations.fit_for_betting_repo: Useful comparison point only. Not suitable for this Python repo spike without explicit runtime authorization.
candidate_evaluations.maintenance_release_evidence: CRAN/GitHub package is public and known in the baseball analytics ecosystem, but it introduces an R runtime dependency.
candidate_evaluations.purpose: R package for baseball data retrieval and analysis across MLB public sources.
candidate_evaluations.python_compatibility: Not a Python library. Introducing R runtime is outside this task unless separately authorized by CTO/Owner.

dependency_policy: No dependency files were modified. Optional import pattern only; dependency additions require Owner authorization.
disclaimer: Historical/read-only MLB data adoption spike only. Not live predictions, not betting advice.
production_policy: No DB writes, no paid provider calls, no live odds calls, no production activation, no future-ticket mutation.
recommended_minimum_path: Use pybaseball as the first optional read-only adapter for historical/statcast samples; consider MLB-StatsAPI later for team/schedule/player metadata after dependency and MLB terms authorization.
task: P211-A Open-Source MLB Data Library Adoption Spike
validation_notes: Adapter fetch methods raise AdapterUnavailableError with clear reasons when packages are missing.
Audit script performs deterministic diagnostics only and does not call live MLB endpoints.
All output is labeled historical/read-only and excludes wagering-performance or staking claims.
